title: Hugelia brauntonii
taxonomy: Hugelia brauntonii is a plant species within the family Cistaceae. The genus Hugelia is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species. This species is native to South America, specifically in the Andean region. The classification of Hugelia brauntonii is still being researched and may be subject to change as new information becomes available. Further studies are needed to confirm its exact classification.

intro: Hugelia brauntonii, also known as Devil's-bit scabious, is a perennial plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, Devil's-bit scabious is used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory problems, such as asthma and bronchitis, and skin conditions like eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of Hugelia brauntonii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Devil's-bit scabious is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to relieve respiratory symptoms and soothe skin irritations.
